bio.extra_names: Find a Grave records as 'John Erik Back' (Memorial 75874861). OAC Baeck Family Papers as 'John Eric Baeck.' Bäck (Swedish) → Baeck (Americanized) likely original. 'Erik' (Wikitree) and 'Eric' (OAC) both attested.

burial.plot_notes: Cypress Lawn Memorial Park, Colma CA — Baeck/Rearden family plot

narrative_blocks.header: Swedish immigrant who married a Berkeley schoolteacher
narrative_blocks.body: Born in Floda, a small village in the Swedish Dalecarlia region, John emigrated to the United States and settled in the San Francisco Bay Area. He married Anna Wooster Rearden in 1917 — a UC Berkeley Class of 1912 schoolteacher — and the couple lived in Berkeley nearly continuously until his 1960 death.

sources.short: OAC Baeck Family Papers finding aid

bio.extra_names: Find a Grave records as 'John Erik Back' (Memorial 75874861). OAC Baeck Family Papers as 'John Eric Baeck.' Bäck (Swedish) → Baeck (Americanized) likely original. 'Erik' (Wikitree) and 'Eric' (OAC) both attested.

narrative_blocks.header: Swedish immigrant who married a Berkeley schoolteacher
narrative_blocks.body: Born in Floda, a small village in the Swedish Dalecarlia region, John emigrated to the United States and settled in the San Francisco Bay Area. He married Anna Wooster Rearden in 1917 — a UC Berkeley Class of 1912 schoolteacher — and the couple lived in Berkeley nearly continuously until his 1960 death.

sources.short: OAC Baeck Family Papers finding aid

bio.extra_names: Also documented as 'Nancy Reardon Back/Baeck' on Find a Grave Memorial 75874428 and Wikitree (Reardon-1094) — likely informal family usage. OAC Baeck Family Papers (Bancroft Library) finding aid uses 'Anna Wooster Rearden Baeck' as the formal name; that archive is the project's primary source for this entry.

burial.plot_notes: Cypress Lawn Memorial Park, Colma — buried with husband John Erik Baeck and daughter Anne Charlotta. Per Find a Grave Memorial 75874428.

education.institution: Public schools of California and Illinois
education.notes: Per OAC Baeck Family Papers finding aid.

Class of 1912. Member of Prytanean Society (UC Berkeley women's honor society). Wrote the first 'Partheneia' to be given at the university by women students — performed in 1912, the year of her graduation.

career.title: Schoolteacher
career.notes: Taught school for several years after graduation, before her 1917 marriage. Per OAC finding aid.

narrative_blocks.header: Wrote UC Berkeley's first women-led Partheneia
narrative_blocks.body: A Class of 1912 graduate of UC Berkeley and member of the Prytanean Society women's honor society, Anna wrote the first Partheneia to be staged at the university by women students. It was performed her graduation year. The Partheneia became a Berkeley tradition that would run through the 1930s.

narrative_blocks.header: Berkeley resident from college through her death
narrative_blocks.body: After teaching school for several years, Anna married John Erik Baeck in 1917, settled in Berkeley, and remained there almost continuously until her death in 1970. Her papers and her father Judge Timothy Rearden's papers were donated to the Bancroft Library at UC Berkeley as the Baeck Family Papers (BANC MSS 74/101 c).

sources.short: OAC Baeck Family Papers finding aid (Bancroft Library, UC Berkeley)
Find a Grave Memorial 75874428 (under name 'Nancy Reardon Back')

burial.plot_notes: Cypress Lawn Memorial Park, Colma CA — Baeck/Rearden family plot

burialCemetery.name: Cypress Lawn Memorial Park
burialCemetery.notes: Founded 1892 Colma CA. Hooker-tree connections will be wired in Stage 6W-2: Hon. Samuel Cowles (HD3710), Anna Wooster Cowles (I00412), most of their seven children, Timothy H. Rearden (I00402), and Rearden-Baeck descendants.
